content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test URL, robots, sitemap, state, scheduling, extraction, dedup, report, and recovery invariants with deterministic clocks and schedules.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Pure Model Tests model · Theory Pure Model Tests, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 9306 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove original fixtures, pure models, compile-negative tests, loopback faults, and mutation detection; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one HTML repair, Cheerio mode, selector, unknown value, record schema, link relation, or metadata case and retain rejected evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test URL, robots, sitemap, state, scheduling, extraction, dedup, report, and recovery invariants with deterministic clocks and schedules.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Pure Model Tests model · Theory Pure Model Tests, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Only end-to-end browser tests reveal meaningful defects.
